notes: A75 landed A73 recommendation #2: removed the two redundant `copy_from_slice` calls inside `CfrPlusVector::walk`'s action loop. On a Hero action the code used to SIMD-multiply `next_hero_take[c] = reach_hero[c] * p[c]` and then `next_villain_take.copy_from_slice(reach_villain)` — a 1326-f32 linear copy of an unchanged buffer — before recursing with `(&next_hero_take, &next_villain_take)`. Symmetric on the Villain branch. The fix is to pass the caller's `reach_villain` / `reach_hero` slice straight through to the recursive call (they're `&[f32]` and the non-acting player's reach is literally unchanged at a decision node), skipping the memmove entirely. No scratch-layout refactor needed; 10-line surgical edit. All correctness gates green: Kuhn 3-way vector_equivalence, NLHE river_vector_vs_flat differential (3 tests), river_canonical (4 scenarios), scripts/e2e.sh (FFI + Swift harness, 13 assertions). `river_canonical_spot` drops 32.87 -> 32.09 ms @ 100 iters (2.4 % faster). `river_wet_board` drops 35.50 -> 34.29 ms (3.4 % faster). `river_degenerate_spot` drops 9.23 -> 8.83 ms @ 1000 iters (4.3 % faster). Flat path `river_canonical_spot_flat` drops 685.81 -> 659.02 ms (3.9 %; hardware-noise drift, flat path code untouched by A75). A73's 8 % estimate was optimistic — A74's walk-SIMD landed first and proportionally reduced the compute-loop cost, so the fixed-cost copy phase became a smaller slice of walk time. The gain is real and stacked but the 30 ms @ 100 iters (300 ms @ 1000 iters) ideal target remains ~2 ms / ~21 ms away. A73 rec #3 (node_util aggregation fusion across actions) is the open follow-up for closing the gap.

river_canonical_spot.notes: AhKhQh2d4s, hero AA/KK/AKs, villain 22+/AJs+/KQs, pot 100, stack 500. 2.4 % faster than A74's 32.87 ms, 21.2 % faster than A70's 40.70 ms. Extrapolates to ~321 ms @ 1000 iters. Still ~21 ms over the 300 ms ideal; 30 ms @ 100 iters target missed by ~2 ms. A73 rec #3 is the remaining lever.

river_degenerate_spot.notes: 2c7d9hTsJs, hero AhKh vs villain AsAd, pot 1000, stack 0. 4.3 % faster than A74's 9.23 ms, 32.2 % faster than A70's 13.02 ms. The trivial spot still pays the 1326-wide reach-vector walk cost but now doesn't pay the redundant copy phase on top of A74's SIMD.

river_wet_board.notes: JhTh9c8h7s, hero AA/AKs/QTs, villain 22+/AQs+, pot 100, stack 500. 3.4 % faster than A74's 35.50 ms. Extrapolates to ~343 ms @ 1000 iters, well under the 500 ms target.

river_canonical_spot_flat.notes: Scalar `CfrPlusFlat` path not touched by A75 (the reach-copy removal is in `CfrPlusVector::walk`, a different function entirely). 659 ms is a hardware-noise drift below A74's 685 ms snapshot; same code, single-run captures vary. Kept as a regression guard.
